note: Texas has 254 counties, each with a partisan-elected sheriff serving a four-year term (most current terms began January 2025 after the November 2024 election); this dataset was gathered in two alphabetical halves (A-K and L-Z), and the L-Z half required extensive per-county verification because published directories were badly outdated — dozens of 2024 election upsets and mid-term vacancies produced new sheriffs (e.g., Montgomery/Doolittle, Williamson/Lindemann, San Jacinto/Sam Houston, Wise/Mellema, Orange/Bobby Smith, Shelby/Corbell), and several seats were filled by appointment after a death, resignation, or retirement (e.g., Wichita/William Rutledge, Martin/Cozart, Robertson/Ruland, Mills/James Jones).
